title: Students - Canada.ca
content:
Topics/services specifically for students. For topics of interest to everyone, see Income tax .
If you were a student or received a scholarship or other study grants in 2025, the Guide P105, Students and Income Tax , will give you helpful information about filing your 2025 Income Tax and Benefit Return.
If you are in Canada as an international student, go to Taxes for International students studying in Canada .
If you were a student who was enrolled at a foreign university, college, or other post-secondary educational institution outside Canada, go to Recognized educational institutions outside Canada .
If you are a student with a disability, go to Persons with disabilities, their caregivers, and the CRA or see Guide RC4064, Disability-Related Information . That guide has information about services and programs that may benefit you, and deductions and credits that may apply to you.
- Who has to file a return Why file a return (even if you have no tax to pay)?
- What documents do you include with your return and what records do you keep What documents do you include with your paper return?
- Common types of income for students Scholarships, research grants, payments from an RESP, tips, and more
- Common deductions and credits for students Tuition, education, and textbook amounts, interest paid on student loans, and moving expenses
- Moving Deductible moving expenses, change of address, and more
- Representative authorization You may authorize a parent or another person to represent you or get information on your tax matters.
- Free tax clinics Get or provide help to file income tax and benefit returns.
- Canada child benefit Find out if you qualify for the Canada Child Benefit.
- Lifelong Learning Plan Do you know you may be able to withdraw from your RRSP to finance your training or education?
- Learn about your taxes A free online learning tool to help youth, individuals who have never done their taxes before and new residents learn about taxes. It is also a great tool for anyone who needs a refresher.

## Forms and publications
- Guide P105, Students and Income Tax
- Information for Students – Educational Institutions outside Canada
Tune in to find our your tax obligations as a student, and what benefits and credits you may be eligible for.

### Give feedback about this page
Thank you for your feedback.
